Flat feet do not usually cause problems Most children have flat feet when they're young. They usually develop arches on the inner sides of their feet between 3 and 10 years of age.
Flat feet:
do not usually cause any problems should not stop you doing any activities, including sports are rarely a sign of anything serious But sometimes, you may have pain around your ankle or lower leg, or the inner side (fallen arch) of your foot may ache. Your shoes may also wear out quickly.
You do not need to do anything if you or your child have flat feet and they're not causing any problems.
Non-urgent advice: See a GP if you have flat feet and:
